task_completion.description: Verify that the Ardour application is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Digital Audio Workstation interface (showing timeline, tracks, or mixer) is visible on the screen.
The mouse cursor is hovering over, clicking, or selecting the track header specifically labeled 'Audio 1'.

task_completion.description: Activate the track name editing field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track name 'Audio 1' turns into an editable text box, often indicated by a highlighted background, a text cursor (caret), or a pop-up rename dialog.

task_completion.description: Enter the new track name 'Lead Vocals'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Lead Vocals' is visible inside the active text input box for the track name.

task_completion.description: Confirm the track has been successfully renamed to 'Lead Vocals'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text input box is closed, and the track header or mixer strip now clearly displays 'Lead Vocals' as the permanent track name.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour graphical user interface to rename the track.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse movements, clicks, and GUI text inputs are visible. There should be no terminal windows open executing scripts to modify the Ardour session file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Ardour application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is clearly the Ardour DAW (showing transport controls, timeline, track headers) and not a text editor modifying the session's XML file.

integrity.description: The agent renamed the existing track instead of creating a new one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original 'Audio 1' track is no longer present, having been replaced by 'Lead Vocals'. There should not be both an 'Audio 1' track and a new 'Lead Vocals' track.
